structured.description: A `Shader` class that loads and manages shaders for a 3D graphics program using the OpenGL API. The class provides methods for binding the shader program, uniform floating-point values, and matrix transformations. It also includes utility methods for loading and validating shader source code. Overall, the code enables developers to create and manage shaders for their 3D graphics applications with ease.

items.description: from the file provides a way to load and manage shaders for use in a graphics program. It allows for the creation of a program object and uniform values can be set for matrices and floats. Additionally, it provides a method to bind the program and get the location of a specified uniform.
items.name: Shader

items.description: glues a program to a specific context, allowing it to be used for rendering and other graphics operations.
items.name: bind

items.description: returns the value of the `program` field of the current instance.

returns.description: an integer representation of the program.

items.name: getProgram

items.description: is a method that takes a string name and an array of float values as parameters. It then uses the `glUniformf()` method to set the specified uniform value(s) for the named location in the current GL context. The length of the input array determines which `glUniformf()` method is called, with longer arrays being handled by more complex versions of the method.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of the uniform location in the program's graphics handling state, which determines where to write the corresponding uniform value(s) using GL20.glUniform() method calls.

items.name: uniformf

items.description: passes a matrix object to OpenGL using the `glUniformMatrix4` method, with the location index specified by the name parameter and the buffer flag set to false.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of a uniform buffer location where the provided matrix will be stored.

params.name: mat
params.description:
4x4 floating-point matrix to be uniformed.

* `getLocation(name)` is used to retrieve the location object for the uniform named `name`. This location object provides the necessary information to bind the uniform to a shader program.
* `glUniformMatrix4()` is used to set a 4x4 matrix as a uniform in a shader program. The matrix data is provided by `Util.createFlippedBuffer(mat.getLinearData())`, which creates a buffer object from the linear data of the `mat` matrix.
* `false` is passed as the second argument to indicate that the uniform should be bound as a column-major matrix, rather than a row-major matrix.

usage.description: Here, "shader" is a Shader object created using the loadShader method with "shader.frag" as the filename, which contains the GLSL code for the shader. The "my_matrix" variable in the GLSL code corresponds to the Matrix4f object passed to the uniformMat4 method.

items.name: unifromMat4

items.description: retrieves the location of a uniform named "name" within a program specified by the `program` parameter using the `GL20.glGetUniformLocation()` method.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of the uniform location to retrieve in the program's uniform buffer.

returns.description: an integer representing the uniform location within a program.

usage.description: The example code provided gets the uniform location of a specified shader variable 'name' in the program 'program'. The method returns an integer representing the location of the variable in memory, which can be used to set values for the variable using other methods like GL20.glUniform1f().

items.name: getLoaction

items.description: loads a shader from a file, creates a program object in OpenGL, and returns a new shader object containing the loaded shader.

params.name: filename
params.description: path to a file containing the vertex and fragment shaders to be loaded into the program.

returns.description:
a new `Shader` object representing a shader program created by combining a vertex shader and a fragment shader.

* The function returns a new Shader object, which represents a shader program that can be used for rendering 3D graphics.
* The Shader object has a reference to the program ID generated by GL20.glCreateProgram(), which is used to identify the shader program.
* The Shader object can be validated using the validateProgram() method, which checks if the shader program is properly linked and ready for use.

Overall, the `loadShader` function is responsible for loading and creating a shader program from a given filename, and returning it as a new Shader object that can be used for rendering purposes.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: creates a new shader object using the `glCreateShader()` method, then sets the source code for the shader using `glShaderSource()`, compiles the shader using `glCompileShader()`, and attaches the shader to a program using `glAttachShader()`.

params.name: target
params.description: 3D graphics context's current active program, which is used to determine the type of shader to create and attach to the program.

params.name: src
params.description: source code of the shader to be compiled.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program to which the loaded shader will be attached.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: validates a program by linking and validating it with the GPU, checking for errors and displaying any that occur.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D program that needs to be validated, and it is passed through various gl* functions for linking and validation.

items.name: validateProgram

items.description: reads the contents of a file and returns its text as a string.

params.name: file
params.description: file to read and contains its path.

returns.description: a string containing the contents of the specified file.

items.name: getText

structured.description: a class `Model` that represents a 3D model in an OpenGL context. It provides a constructor to set the vertex array object (VAO) and the number of vertices, and a method `draw()` to render the model. The `enableAttribs()` and `disableAttribs()` methods enable and disable specific vertex attributes, respectively. The `getVAO()` and `getSize()` methods provide accessors for the VAO and size, respectively. Additionally, the code provides a factory method `load()` to load a 3D model from an array of vertices and an array of indices.

items.description: is used to handle vertex and index buffer data for rendering 3D models in OpenGL. It provides methods for binding and unbinding Vertex Array Objects (VAOs) and Element Array Buffers (EABs), as well as storing vertex and index data and enabling/disabling vertex attributtes. The class also provides a way to load pre-defined model data from an array of vertices and indices.
items.name: Model

items.description: binds a vertex array object, enables vertex attributes for position and texture coord, draws triangles using `glDrawElements`, disables vertex attributes, and unbinds the vertex array object.
items.name: draw

items.description: enables two vertex attributes in a GL context using `glEnableVertexAttribArray`.
items.name: enableAttribs

items.description: disables both vertex attribute arrays for a specific GL context.
items.name: disableAttribs

items.description: returns the value of the variable `vao`.

returns.description: an integer value representing the VAO.

usage.code:
Model model = Model.load(vertices, indices);
model.getVAO(); // returns the VAO value

items.name: getVAO

items.description: retrieves the value of a field named `size`.
items.name: getSize

items.description: loads a 3D model from an array of vertices and an array of indices. It creates a Vertex Array Object (VAO), stores the indices, and then binds and unbinds the VAO. Finally, it returns a `Model` object representing the loaded model.

params.name: vertices
params.description:
3D geometric data of the model to be loaded, which is stored in an array of floating-point values.

* `vertices`: An array of floating-point values representing the 3D vertices of a model.
* `indices`: An array of integers representing the indices of the vertices in the model.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D model's vertex indices, which are used to bind the vertices of the model to its corresponding geometry in the graphics pipeline.

* `indices`: An `int[]` array containing the indices of the vertices in the model.
* `v_count`: The number of vertices in the model, which can be calculated by summing up the lengths of all the `indices` arrays.

returns.description:
a `Model` object representing the loaded 3D model.

* `Model`: This is the type of the output, which is an instance of the `Model` class.
* `vao`: This is an integer variable representing the VAO (Vertex Array Object) handle created by the function.
* `v_count`: This is an integer variable representing the number of vertices in the model.

items.name: load

items.description: generates a new vertex array object (Vao) and binds it to the current context, allowing for its use in rendering graphics.

returns.description: an integer value representing the unique vertex array object (VAO) created.

items.name: createVAO

items.description: stores floating-point array data into a buffer object and sets vertex attributes for rendering.

params.name: attrib
params.description: 3D vertex attribute index that stores the data for the current buffer in the GPU's memory.

params.name: data
params.description:
2D array of floating-point values that will be stored in the vertex buffer object (VBO).

* `data`: An array of 4 float values representing vertex data.
* `attrib`: An integer representing the attribute index to which the vertex data will be mapped. There are two attribute indices in total, assigned using the `GL20.glVertexAttribPointer()` method.
* `vbo`: A buffer object handle generated by `GL15.glGenBuffers()`. The buffer is bound using `GL15.glBindBuffer(GL15.GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, vbo)` and data is transferred to it using `GL15.glBufferData(GL15.GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, Util.createFlippedBuffer(data), GL15.GL_STATIC_DRAW)`.

items.name: storeData

items.description: genrates a new buffer object and binds it to an element array buffer, then stores the provided integer array data in the buffer using the `glBufferData` method.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D indices of the vertices of a geometric shape, which are stored in a buffer for later use in rendering.

* `indices` is an integer array representing a buffer containing indices for an element array buffer.
* The length of the array is specified by the size of the `indices` array, which is a variable passed as a parameter to the function.
* Each element in the `indices` array represents an index into a buffer for an element array, with each index being an integer value between 0 and the total number of elements in the buffer - 1, inclusive.

items.name: storeIndices

items.description: binds a vertex array object (VAO) using the `GL30.glBindVertexArray()` method, setting it to null.
items.name: unbindVAO
